steps.title: Turn the condition into an equation
steps.content:
The condition $f(a)=-9a$ gives
$$\sqrt{6a+7}=-9a$$
The right side must be nonnegative, so $a\le0$.

steps.title: Check the negative choice
steps.content:
Test $a=-\frac{7}{27}$. Inside the square root,
$$6(-\frac{7}{27})+7=\frac{49}{9}$$
so the left side is $\frac{7}{3}$. The right side is
$$-9(-\frac{7}{27})=\frac{7}{3}$$
The answer is B.
